allies.rationale_text: Systems-thinking helps correlation-vs-causation map relations and context so the team can reason about causes instead of reading isolated symptoms.
allies.source_quote: Systems thinking demands attention to relations and contexts, and the ultimate goal is to understand the underlying system of an area of expertise, including the nature of cause and effect.

allies.rationale_text: Experimentation gives correlation-vs-causation a disciplined way to test whether the claimed driver actually produces the observed result.
allies.source_quote: Understanding causation drivers leads toward statistical analysis and experiments.

allies.rationale_text: Root-cause-analysis complements correlation-vs-causation by pushing the team past the proximate event toward the deeper mechanism that generated it.
allies.source_quote: This involves striving to separate the proximate cause (what happened immediately before) from the root cause (the true underlying driver).

antagonists.rationale_text: Confirmation-bias interferes with causal diagnosis by filtering evidence toward the cause story the team already wants to believe.
antagonists.source_quote: Confirmation Bias ... directly interferes with accurate causal analysis.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Correlation-vs-causation conflicts with confirmation-bias when observed correlations are selectively elevated to causal status because they confirm a pre-existing theory.
structured_tensions.source_quote: correlation-vs-causation conflicts with confirmation-bias (confirmation bias) when observed correlations are selectively elevated to causal status because they confirm a pre-existing theory.

structured_tensions.tension_text: Correlation-vs-causation conflicts with simplification when insistence on establishing causation blocks timely action in situations where strong correlation is the best available decision input.
structured_tensions.source_quote: correlation-vs-causation conflicts with simplification (simplification) when insistence on establishing causation blocks timely action in situations where strong correlation is the best available decision input.

curation_notes.summary: The raw source is strongest on mechanism-tracing allies and one clean anti-bias conflict rather than on an explicit structured tension block.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor relation sprawl where the raw source mainly supports systems, experimentation, and root-cause links.
open_questions: Fast-thinking and availability pressure are source-real antagonists, but confirmation-bias was the cleanest bounded fit into the current relation surface.
